senses.definition: used to imply that achieving the desired outcome is more important than superficial qualities or traditional approaches, highlighting the importance of practicality and effectiveness

word_list.word: a king's chaff is worth more than other men's corn

senses.definition: used to imply that something associated with a person in a position of power is perceived as more valuable than the same thing associated with someone of lesser status, regardless of its actual worth
used to highlight the idea of keeping or storing items that may not have immediate value or purpose, as they could prove useful or valuable in the future

word_list.word: free vinegar is sweeter than honey

senses.definition: used to suggest that something that is free is often more appreciated than something that is expensive, even if the free thing is of lower quality

senses.definition: used to imply that even seemingly insignificant things or people can have value or usefulness, and that one should not dismiss or underestimate them too quickly
used to imply that something or someone is completely worthless and has no value at all

examples.x: The lazy employee's work was not worth a brass farthing, and he was eventually fired for his lack of effort.

senses.definition: used to suggest that what is considered worthless by one person may be valuable to another, as value is subjective and varies from person to person

senses.definition: used to suggest that if one holds on to something for a long enough time, one will eventually find a purpose or a use for it

word_list.word: no more than the man in the moon
word_list.phonetic_am_text: nooo mawr dhńĀn dhńď mńĀn in dhńď moon

senses.definition: used to imply that someone or something is considered to be insignificant, unimportant, or of little value

examples.x: The company's latest product was hyped up in the media, but when it was released, it turned out to be no more than the man in the moon - it didn't live up to the promises made in the advertising.
